title: Debug Failing CI/CD Pipeline
description: A CI/CD pipeline is failing intermittently. Diagnose the root cause and fix the configuration.
scenario: Your team's deployment pipeline has been failing 40% of the time for the past 3 days. Review the pipeline configuration and error logs to identify and fix all issues.
pipelineConfig:
name: Deploy to Production
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test
        
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Build Docker image
        run: docker build -t myapp:latest .
      - name: Push to registry
        run: |
          docker login -u $DOCKER_USER -p $DOCKER_PASS
          docker push myapp:latest
          
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to server
        run: ssh deploy@prod-server 'docker pull myapp:latest && docker restart myapp'
errorLogs: ERROR: npm test - ECONNRESET - network timeout fetching test dependencies
ERROR: docker push - unauthorized: authentication required (intermittent)
ERROR: ssh: connect to host prod-server port 22: Connection timed out
ERROR: Build job ran before test job completed successfully
questions: List all issues in this pipeline configuration.
Why is the deploy job sometimes running even when tests fail?
Rewrite the pipeline with proper job dependencies and fixes.
What secrets management improvements would you make?
How would you add rollback capability to this pipeline?

title: Production Incident Response
description: Respond to a production incident using observability data.
scenario: It's 3pm on a Friday. Your e-commerce site is experiencing degraded performance. Orders are failing. You have 15 minutes to diagnose and mitigate. Review the observability data below.

recentChanges: 2:45pm - Traffic spike from marketing email campaign
topSlowQueries: SELECT * FROM products p JOIN recommendations r ON p.id = r.product_id WHERE r.user_id = ? -- avg 2,100ms
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL 30 DAY -- avg 890ms (no index)
observabilityData.errorSample: Error: Connection pool exhausted - all 500 connections in use

questions: What is the root cause of this incident? Walk through your reasoning.
What are your immediate mitigation steps (next 15 minutes)?
What is the permanent fix for each identified issue?
How would you write the post-mortem for this incident?
What monitoring/alerting would have caught this earlier?
evaluationCriteria: Identifies N+1 query from recommendation feature as root cause
Suggests immediate: scale up, disable feature flag, increase connection pool
Suggests proactive load testing before deployments
